content.text:
<user_query>
You are an expert full-stack developer. Build a complete Progressive Web App (PWA) for a betting/data entry system with three user roles: Super Admin, Admin, and User.
Core Requirements:
User Roles & Permissions:
Super Admin: Can do everything admin can do
get the notification when he get the message from the admin.
can see the message sent from admin and read it.
Create and delete Admins and Users
Assign username and password
View all data from all users
Delete any user
Admin:
can send a message to superadmin only 1 text box and submit button
Can view only users created by him and their submitted data
Can filter data by date and by user only he created
Can create user(assign username and password) and can delete user only created by him
can switch on/off the user's ability to edit his entry data
User:
Can only access his own dashboard
Can submit new entries
Can view and edit his own previous entries when he is allowed by his admin
Data Entry Form (User Side):
Each entry contains:
Date (default today, editable)
System (number)
Online(number)
Number(number) if it is not filled by default it will be zero.
Bonus (number)
Win (number)
Cash (number)
Note (textarea, optional)
Dashboard Features:
User Dashboard:
Clean form to submit new entry
History table of all his previous entries (with edit button if he is allowed by his admin)
Cannot  edit any previous entry unless he is allowed by his admin.
Admin Dashboard:
List of all users with edit switch button to allow or to denies to edit their data 
Click on any user to see their full history
Date filter (single date + date range)
Search by user
Super Admin Dashboard:
All Admin features
User Management (Create Admin/User, Delete User, Reset Password)
Overview of all activity
Tech Stack (Use this exact stack):
Frontend: Next.js 15 (App Router) + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + Shadcn/ui
Backend: Next.js API Routes
Database: Supabase (PostgreSQL) or Prisma + SQLite (for easier local development)
Authentication: NextAuth.js or Clerk (choose the simpler one)
PWA: Must be a full Progressive Web App (installable, offline support, manifest, service worker)
State Management: Zustand or React Context
Design Requirements:
Modern, clean, dark theme (professional dashboard style)
Fully responsive (mobile + desktop)
Beautiful tables and forms
Loading states and success/error messages
Good UX for data entry
Additional Features:
Secure password handling
Proper role-based access control
Data validation
Responsive sidebar navigation
Logout functionality
Nice dashboard statistics (total entries, total cash, etc. where applicable)
Please generate the complete project structure with all necessary files and clear instructions on how to set it up (including Supabase or database setup).
Start by showing the folder structure, then provide the most important files one by one (starting with layout, auth, and database schema).
Make the code clean, well-commented, and production-ready.
</user_query>

Building the PWA from scratch: checking the workspace, then scaffolding the project structure and core files.

[REDACTED]

Creating the project and scaffolding the full application.

[REDACTED]

todos.content: Auth, middleware, API routes, lib utilities

{
  "name": "betting-data-pwa",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev",
    "build": "prisma generate && next build",
    "start": "next start",
    "lint": "next lint",
    "db:push": "prisma db push",
    "db:seed": "tsx prisma/seed.ts",
    "db:studio": "prisma studio",
    "postinstall": "prisma generate"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@auth/prisma-adapter": "^2.7.4",
    "@hookform/resolvers": "^3.9.1",
    "@prisma/client": "^6.1.0",
    "@radix-ui/react-alert-dialog": "^1.1.4",
    "@radix-ui/react-dialog": "^1.1.4",
    "@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu": "^2.1.4",
    "@radix-ui/react-label": "^2.1.1",
    "@radix-ui/react-select": "^2.1.4",
    "@radix-ui/react-slot": "^1.1.1",
    "@radix-ui/react-switch": "^1.1.2",
    "@radix-ui/react-tabs": "^1.1.2",
    "@radix-ui/react-toast": "^1.2.4",
    "@serwist/next": "^9.0.11",
    "bcryptjs": "^2.4.3",
    "class-variance-authority": "^0.7.1",
    "clsx": "^2.1.1",
    "date-fns": "^4.1.0",
    "lucide-react": "^0.469.0",
    "next": "^15.1.3",
    "next-auth": "^4.24.11",
    "next-themes": "^0.4.4",
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "react-hook-form": "^7.54.2",
    "serwist": "^9.0.11",
    "tailwind-merge": "^2.6.0",
    "tailwindcss-animate": "^1.0.7",
    "zod": "^3.24.1",
    "zustand": "^5.0.2"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/bcryptjs": "^2.4.6",
    "@types/node": "^22.10.2",
    "@types/react": "^19.0.2",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.0.2",
    "eslint": "^9.17.0",
    "eslint-config-next": "^15.1.3",
    "postcss": "^8.4.49",
    "prisma": "^6.1.0",
    "tailwindcss": "^3.4.17",
    "tsx": "^4.19.2",
    "typescript": "^5.7.2"
  }
}

// Betting Data Entry PWA - Database Schema
generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "sqlite"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

enum Role {
  SUPER_ADMIN
  ADMIN
  USER
}

model User {
  id              String    @id @default(cuid())
  username        String    @unique
  passwordHash    String
  role            Role      @default(USER)
  canEditEntries  Boolean   @default(false)
  createdAt       DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime  @updatedAt

  // Admin who created this user (null for super admin / bootstrap users)
  createdById     String?
  createdBy       User?     @relation("UserCreator", fields: [createdById], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  createdUsers    User[]    @relation("UserCreator")

  entries         Entry[]
  sentMessages    Message[] @relation("MessageFromAdmin")

  @@index([role])
  @@index([createdById])
}

model Entry {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String
  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  date      DateTime
  system    Float    @default(0)
  online    Float    @default(0)
  number    Float    @default(0)
  bonus     Float    @default(0)
  win       Float    @default(0)
  cash      Float    @default(0)
  note      String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt

  @@index([userId])
  @@index([date])
}

model Message {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  content   String
  read      Boolean  @default(false)
  adminId   String
  admin     User     @relation("MessageFromAdmin", fields: [adminId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([read])
  @@index([createdAt])
}

# Copy to .env and adjust values
DATABASE_URL="file:./dev.db"
NEXTAUTH_URL="http://localhost:3000"
NEXTAUTH_SECRET="change-this-to-a-long-random-string-in-production"

# Default super admin (created on first seed)
SEED_SUPER_ADMIN_USERNAME="superadmin"
SEED_SUPER_ADMIN_PASSWORD="SuperAdmin123!"

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2017",
    "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext", "webworker"],
    "allowJs": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "strict": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "module": "esnext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "jsx": "preserve",
    "incremental": true,
    "plugins": [{ "name": "next" }],
    "paths": { "@/*": ["./src/*"] },
    "types": ["@serwist/next/typings"]
  },
  "include": ["next-env.d.ts", "**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx", ".next/types/**/*.ts"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "public/sw.js"]
}

# dependencies
/node_modules
/.pnp
.pnp.js
.yarn/install-state.gz

# testing
/coverage

# next.js
/.next/
/out/

# production
/build

# misc
.DS_Store
*.pem

# debug
npm-debug.log*
yarn-debug.log*
yarn-error.log*

# env
.env
.env*.local

# prisma
prisma/dev.db
prisma/dev.db-journal

# typescript
*.tsbuildinfo
next-env.d.ts

# serwist
public/sw.js
public/sw.js.map

{
  "name": "Betting Data Entry",
  "short_name": "BetData",
  "description": "Progressive Web App for betting data entry and management",
  "start_url": "/",
  "display": "standalone",
  "background_color": "#0a0a0f",
  "theme_color": "#6366f1",
  "orientation": "portrait-primary",
  "icons": [
    {
      "src": "/icons/icon-192.png",

    },

  ]

NEXTAUTH_URL="http://localhost:3000"
NEXTAUTH_SECRET="dev-secret-change-in-production-use-openssl-rand"

SEED_SUPER_ADMIN_PASSWORD="SuperAdmin123!"

# PWA Icons

Add `icon-192.png` and `icon-512.png` here (192×192 and 512×512 PNG).

You can generate them from any logo using https://realfavicongenerator.net/ or:

```bash
# Example with ImageMagick (if installed)
convert -size 512x512 xc:'#6366f1' -fill white -gravity center -pointsize 72 -annotate 0 'BD' public/icons/icon-512.png
convert public/icons/icon-512.png -resize 192x192 public/icons/icon-192.png
```

Until icons exist, the PWA may still install but show a generic icon.

# Betting Data Entry PWA

A production-ready Progressive Web App for betting/data entry with three roles: **Super Admin**, **Admin**, and **User**.

## Tech stack

| Layer | Technology |
|--------|------------|
| Frontend | Next.js 15 (App Router), TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn-style UI |
| Backend | Next.js API Routes |
| Database | Prisma + SQLite (local) — PostgreSQL/Supabase ready |
| Auth | NextAuth.js (credentials) |
| PWA | Serwist (service worker + manifest) |
| State | Zustand |

## Folder structure

```
betting-data-pwa/
├── prisma/
│   ├── schema.prisma      # User, Entry, Message models
│   └── seed.ts            # Creates default super admin
├── public/
│   ├── manifest.json      # PWA manifest
│   └── icons/             # 192 & 512 PNG icons
├── src/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── api/
│   │   │   ├── auth/[...nextauth]/
│   │   │   ├── entries/           # CRUD entries
│   │   │   ├── users/             # User management
│   │   │   ├── messages/          # Admin → Super Admin messages
│   │   │   └── stats/             # Dashboard aggregates
│   │   ├── dashboard/
│   │   │   ├── user/              # User: submit + history
│   │   │   ├── admin/             # Admin: users, filters, data
│   │   │   │   └── message/       # Admin message to super admin
│   │   │   └── super-admin/       # Overview, users, messages
│   │   ├── login/
│   │   ├── layout.tsx
│   │   └── globals.css
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── entries/               # Form + table
│   │   ├── layout/                # Sidebar shell
│   │   └── ui/                    # shadcn primitives
│   ├── lib/                       # Auth, Prisma, validation
│   ├── store/                     # Zustand (toasts, sidebar, unread)
│   ├── middleware.ts              # RBAC route protection
│   └── sw.ts                      # Service worker (Serwist)
├── .env.example
├── next.config.ts
└── package.json
```

## Role capabilities

| Feature | User | Admin | Super Admin |
|---------|------|-------|-------------|
| Submit entries | ✅ | — | — |
| Edit own entries | If allowed | — | — |
| View own history | ✅ | — | — |
| Create/delete users | — | Own users only | All admins & users |
| Toggle user edit permission | — | ✅ | ✅ |
| Filter data by date/user | — | Own users | All |
| Message super admin | — | ✅ | — |
| Read admin messages | — | — | ✅ + unread badge |
| Reset passwords | — | Own users | All |

## Quick start (local)

### 1. Install dependencies

```bash
npm install
```

### 2. Environment

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

Edit `.env` — set a strong `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` for production:

```bash
openssl rand -base64 32
```

### 3. Database

```bash
npx prisma db push
npm run db:seed
```

Default super admin (from `.env`):

- **Username:** `superadmin`
- **Password:** `SuperAdmin123!`

### 4. PWA icons (optional but recommended)

Add `public/icons/icon-192.png` and `public/icons/icon-512.png`. See `public/icons/README.md`.

### 5. Run

```bash
npm run dev
```

Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000).

### 6. Production build

```bash
npm run build
npm start
```

Service worker is **enabled in production** (`next build`). In dev it is disabled for easier debugging.

## Using PostgreSQL / Supabase

1. Change `prisma/schema.prisma`:

```prisma
datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}
```

2. Set `DATABASE_URL` in `.env`:

```
DATABASE_URL="postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/dbname?schema=public"
```

3. Run:

```bash
npx prisma db push
npm run db:seed
```

## Entry fields

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| Date | date | Defaults to today |
| System | number | |
| Online | number | |
| Number | number | Defaults to **0** if empty |
| Bonus | number | |
| Win | number | |
| Cash | number | |
| Note | textarea | Optional |

## Security notes

- Passwords hashed with **bcrypt** (12 rounds)
- JWT sessions via NextAuth
- API routes enforce role checks server-side
- Middleware protects `/dashboard/*` routes
- Change default super admin password immediately in production
- Use HTTPS in production for PWA install + secure cookies

## Install as PWA

1. Deploy over HTTPS (or use localhost for testing).
2. Build with `npm run build && npm start`.
3. In Chrome/Edge: address bar → **Install app**.
4. Offline: cached assets via Serwist; API calls require network unless extended.

## Scripts

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm run dev` | Development server |
| `npm run build` | Production build + Prisma generate |
| `npm run db:push` | Sync schema to DB |
| `npm run db:seed` | Create super admin |
| `npm run db:studio` | Prisma Studio GUI |

## License

MIT — use freely for your project.

content.text:
The **Betting Data Entry PWA** is in your workspace at `C:\Users\Abex\.cursor\projects\empty-window`. Below is the structure, what each part does, and how to run it.

## Folder structure

```
empty-window/
├── prisma/
│   ├── schema.prisma          # User, Entry, Message + Role enum
│   └── seed.ts                # Default super admin
├── public/
│   ├── manifest.json          # PWA manifest
│   └── icons/                 # Add icon-192.png & icon-512.png
├── scripts/
│   └── generate-icons.mjs     # Generates PWA icons
├── src/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── api/
│   │   │   ├── auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts
│   │   │   ├── entries/route.ts & [id]/route.ts
│   │   │   ├── users/route.ts & [id]/route.ts
│   │   │   ├── messages/route.ts & [id]/route.ts
│   │   │   └── stats/route.ts
│   │   ├── dashboard/
│   │   │   ├── user/page.tsx
│   │   │   ├── admin/page.tsx & message/page.tsx
│   │   │   └── super-admin/page.tsx, users/, messages/
│   │   ├── login/page.tsx
│   │   ├── layout.tsx
│   │   └── globals.css
│   ├── components/            # UI, forms, tables, sidebar
│   ├── lib/                   # Auth, Prisma, validation, RBAC
│   ├── store/use-app-store.ts # Zustand (toasts, sidebar, unread)
│   ├── middleware.ts
│   └── sw.ts                  # Serwist service worker
├── .env.example
├── next.config.ts             # Serwist PWA wrapper
├── package.json
└── README.md                  # Full setup guide
```

## Setup (run in project folder)

```bash
cd C:\Users\Abex\.cursor\projects\empty-window
npm install
cp .env.example .env   # or copy manually on Windows
npx prisma db push
npm run db:seed
node scripts/generate-icons.mjs
npm run dev
```

**Default Super Admin:** `superadmin` / `SuperAdmin123!`

Open http://localhost:3000

---

## Core files

### Database schema (`prisma/schema.prisma`)

- **User** — username, bcrypt password, role (`SUPER_ADMIN` | `ADMIN` | `USER`), `canEditEntries`, `createdById`
- **Entry** — date, system, online, number (default 0), bonus, win, cash, note
- **Message** — admin → super admin, `read` flag for notifications

### Auth (`src/lib/auth-options.ts`)

- NextAuth **credentials** provider (username + password)
- JWT session with `role`, `canEditEntries`, `id`
- Role-based redirects via `dashboardPathForRole()`

### Middleware (`src/middleware.ts`)

- Protects `/dashboard/*` and API routes
- Super Admin → `/dashboard/super-admin`
- Admin → `/dashboard/admin`
- User → `/dashboard/user`

### PWA

- `public/manifest.json` — installable app metadata
- `@serwist/next` in `next.config.ts` — service worker (production builds)
- Offline caching via Serwist default runtime cache

---

## Role features implemented

| Role | Features |
|------|----------|
| **User** | Submit entries, history table, edit only when `canEditEntries` is on |
| **Admin** | Create/delete own users, edit toggle switch, date/user filters, message to super admin |
| **Super Admin** | All admin data access, user management (create admin/user, delete, reset password), read admin messages with unread badge + polling |

---

## Dashboards

- **`/dashboard/user`** — entry form + history + stats (entries, cash, win, bonus)
- **`/dashboard/admin`** — user list with edit switch, filters, entry table
- **`/dashboard/admin/message`** — single textarea + submit to super admin
- **`/dashboard/super-admin`** — overview stats + recent activity
- **`/dashboard/super-admin/users`** — create admin/user, delete, reset password
- **`/dashboard/super-admin/messages`** — read/mark read admin messages

---

## PostgreSQL / Supabase

Change `provider` to `postgresql` in `prisma/schema.prisma` and set `DATABASE_URL` to your Supabase connection string, then run `npx prisma db push` and `npm run db:seed`.

---

## Production PWA install

1. Set a strong `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` in `.env`
2. `npm run build && npm start`
3. Serve over **HTTPS**
4. Use browser **Install app** (service worker is enabled in production only)

Full details are in `README.md`. If you want, I can add email notifications for new admin messages, export to CSV, or deploy config for Vercel/Railway next.

content.text:
## Most likely cause

**Nothing is listening on port 5000.** `ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED` means the browser reached your machine, but no process accepted the connection.

For this project, the usual reasons are:

1. **The dev server was never started** (most common) — changing port in config does not start Next.js.
2. **`npm install` was never run** — there is no `node_modules` folder in the project, so `npm run dev` would fail immediately.
3. **The server crashed on startup** — wrong directory, missing Node, or Prisma/DB errors.

Your terminal is at `C:\Users\Abex`, not the project folder, and no dev server appears to be running.

---

## Fastest checks (in order)

### 1. Confirm something is on port 5000

In PowerShell:

```powershell
netstat -ano | findstr :5000
```

- **No output** → nothing is running → start the app (step 3).
- **A line with `LISTENING`** → something is bound; try `http://127.0.0.1:5000` or check firewall.

### 2. Go to the project folder

```powershell
cd C:\Users\Abex\.cursor\projects\empty-window
```

### 3. Install dependencies (required once)

```powershell
npm install
```

You should see a `node_modules` folder when this finishes. If `node` or `npm` is not found, install Node.js LTS from https://nodejs.org.

### 4. Set up the database (first time only)

```powershell
npx prisma db push
npm run db:seed
```

### 5. Start the dev server

```powershell
npm run dev
```

Wait until you see something like:

```text
▲ Next.js ...
- Local: http://localhost:5000
```

Only then open **http://localhost:5000**.

### 6. If `npm run dev` fails

Read the error in the terminal:

| Error | Fix |
|--------|-----|
| `'next' is not recognized` | Run `npm install` in the project folder |
| Prisma / database errors | Run `npx prisma db push` |
| Port in use | Stop the other process or use another port |

---

## Quick checklist

| Check | Expected |
|--------|----------|
| `node -v` | e.g. `v20.x` or `v22.x` |
| `Test-Path node_modules` | `True` |
| `npm run dev` running | Terminal stays open, shows `localhost:5000` |
| `netstat` on `:5000` | `LISTENING` |

---

## Summary

The port config (`5000` in `package.json` and `.env`) is fine. The connection is refused because **the Next.js dev server is almost certainly not running**, and **dependencies likely were never installed**.

Run these in order in `C:\Users\Abex\.cursor\projects\empty-window`:

```powershell
npm install
npx prisma db push
npm run db:seed
npm run dev
```

Then reload **http://localhost:5000**. If `npm run dev` prints an error, paste that output here and we can fix the next issue.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
## Error Type
Runtime TypeError

## Error Message
Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'custom')

    at (middleware)/./node_modules/openid-client/lib/device_flow_handle.js (.next\server\middleware.js:2885:1)
    at __webpack_require__ (.next\server\edge-runtime-webpack.js:37:33)
    at fn (.next\server\edge-runtime-webpack.js:315:21)
    at (middleware)/./node_modules/openid-client/lib/client.js (.next\server\middleware.js:2875:1)
    at __webpack_require__ (.next\server\edge-runtime-webpack.js:37:33)
    at fn (.next\server\edge-runtime-webpack.js:315:21)
    at (middleware)/./node_modules/openid-client/lib/issuer.js (.next\server\middleware.js:3115:1)

Next.js version: 15.5.18 (Webpack)
</user_query>

NextAuth's `withAuth` middleware pulls `openid-client` into the Edge runtime, which causes this error. Inspecting the middleware and switching to an Edge-compatible auth check.

[REDACTED]

The middleware imports `withAuth` and `@/lib/auth`, which pulls `openid-client` and Prisma into the Edge runtime. Replacing it with Edge-safe `getToken` from `next-auth/jwt` and isolating role routing.

[REDACTED]

## Cause

`next-auth/middleware`’s `withAuth` bundles **`openid-client`**, which is not compatible with the **Edge runtime** used by Next.js middleware. That leads to:

`Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'custom')`

Importing `@/lib/auth` in middleware also risked pulling in heavier NextAuth/Prisma code.

## Fix

Middleware was rewritten to use only **`getToken` from `next-auth/jwt`**, which is Edge-safe. Role routing lives in `src/lib/dashboard-path.ts` with no Prisma or `openid-client` imports.

## What you should do

1. Stop the dev server (Ctrl+C).
2. Clear the build cache (recommended):

```powershell
cd C:\Users\Abex\.cursor\projects\empty-window
Remove-Item -Recurse -Force .next -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
```

3. Start again:

```powershell
npm run dev
```

4. Open **http://localhost:5000** again.

Auth behavior is unchanged: protected routes still require a valid session, and role-based redirects still apply. If anything else shows up in the terminal after restart, paste it here.

[REDACTED]

input.new_string:
## PWA (Progressive Web App)

This app is a full PWA with:

- **Web app manifest** — `src/app/manifest.ts` (served at `/manifest.webmanifest`)
- **Service worker** — Serwist (`src/sw.ts` → `public/sw.js`)
- **Offline fallback** — `/~offline` when navigation fails without network
- **Install prompt** — banner on supported browsers (Chrome, Edge, etc.)
- **Icons** — `public/icons/icon-192.png` & `icon-512.png` (run `npm run icons`)

### Test locally

```bash
npm run icons          # generate icons if missing
npm run dev            # SW enabled in dev (Serwist disable: false)
```

Open **http://localhost:5000** → DevTools → **Application** → Manifest / Service Workers.

For production:

```bash
npm run build && npm start
```

### Install on device

1. Use **HTTPS** in production (localhost is fine for dev).
2. Chrome/Edge: address bar → **Install BetData** (or use the in-app install banner).
3. iOS Safari: **Share** → **Add to Home Screen**.

API calls (login, save entries) still need network; static UI and cached pages work offline.

content.text:
The app is now configured as a **full PWA**. Here’s what was added or changed:

## PWA features enabled

| Feature | Implementation |
|--------|----------------|
| **Manifest** | `src/app/manifest.ts` → `/manifest.webmanifest` |
| **Service worker** | Serwist (`src/sw.ts`), enabled in **dev and production** |
| **SW registration** | `PwaProvider` (`@serwist/next/react`) |
| **Offline page** | `/~offline` when navigation fails offline |
| **Install banner** | `InstallPrompt` component |
| **Icons** | `public/icon.svg` + PNGs via `npm run icons` |
| **Apple / mobile** | `apple-touch-icon`, `appleWebApp`, `viewportFit` |

## Restart required

```powershell
cd C:\Users\Abex\.cursor\projects\empty-window
Remove-Item -Recurse -Force .next -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
npm run icons
npm run dev
```

## Verify PWA

1. Open **http://localhost:5000**
2. DevTools → **Application** tab:
   - **Manifest** — name, icons, `display: standalone`
   - **Service workers** — `/sw.js` registered and running
3. Use the **Install BetData** banner (Chrome/Edge) or the install icon in the address bar
4. Test offline: DevTools → Network → **Offline**, reload → you should see the offline page for navigations

## Notes

- **Login and API** still need network; the shell and cached assets work offline.
- **iOS**: Share → **Add to Home Screen** (no install banner on Safari).
- **Production**: `npm run build && npm start` over HTTPS for the best install experience.

If the service worker doesn’t appear, hard-refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) or clear site data once after restart.

export default defineCloudflareConfig({});

/_next/static/*
  Cache-Control: public,max-age=31536000,immutable

/icons/*
  Cache-Control: public,max-age=604800

/sw.js
  Cache-Control: no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate

-- Prisma schema initial migration for Cloudflare D1 (SQLite)

-- CreateTable
CREATE TABLE "User" (
    "id" TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,
    "username" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "passwordHash" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "role" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'USER',
    "canEditEntries" BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
    "createdAt" DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    "updatedAt" DATETIME NOT NULL,
    "createdById" TEXT,
    CONSTRAINT "User_createdById_fkey" FOREIGN KEY ("createdById") REFERENCES "User" ("id") ON DELETE SET NULL ON UPDATE CASCADE
);

-- CreateTable

    "id" TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,
    "userId" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "date" DATETIME NOT NULL,
    "system" REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    "online" REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    "number" REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    "bonus" REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    "win" REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    "cash" REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    "note" TEXT,
    "createdAt" DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    "updatedAt" DATETIME NOT NULL,
    CONSTRAINT "Entry_userId_fkey" FOREIGN KEY ("userId") REFERENCES "User" ("id") ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE
);

-- CreateTable
CREATE TABLE "Message" (
    "id" TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,
    "content" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "read" BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
    "adminId" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "createdAt" DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    CONSTRAINT "Message_adminId_fkey" FOREIGN KEY ("adminId") REFERENCES "User" ("id") ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE
);

-- CreateIndex
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX "User_username_key" ON "User"("username");
CREATE INDEX "User_role_idx" ON "User"("role");
CREATE INDEX "User_createdById_idx" ON "User"("createdById");
CREATE INDEX "Entry_userId_idx" ON "Entry"("userId");
CREATE INDEX "Entry_date_idx" ON "Entry"("date");
CREATE INDEX "Message_read_idx" ON "Message"("read");
CREATE INDEX "Message_createdAt_idx" ON "Message"("createdAt");

input.contents:
# Copy to .dev.vars for local Cloudflare Workers preview (wrangler)
NEXTJS_ENV=development
NEXTAUTH_URL=http://localhost:8787
NEXTAUTH_SECRET=dev-secret-change-in-production

# Deploy to Cloudflare Workers

This app uses **[@opennextjs/cloudflare](https://opennext.js.org/cloudflare)** and **Cloudflare D1** (SQLite at the edge). Local file SQLite does not work on Workers.

## Prerequisites

1. [Cloudflare account](https://dash.cloudflare.com/sign-up) (free tier works)
2. Node.js 18+
3. Log in to Wrangler:

```bash
npx wrangler login
```

## 1. Install dependencies

```bash
npm install
```

## 2. Create D1 database

```bash
npx wrangler d1 create betting-data-db
```

Copy the `database_id` from the output into `wrangler.jsonc`:

```jsonc
"database_id": "YOUR_DATABASE_ID_HERE"
```

## 3. Apply database migrations

**Local (preview):**

```bash
npx wrangler d1 migrations apply betting-data-db --local
```

**Production (remote):**

```bash
npx wrangler d1 migrations apply betting-data-db --remote
```

## 4. Seed super admin on D1

```bash
node scripts/generate-seed-sql.mjs
npx wrangler d1 execute betting-data-db --remote --file=scripts/seed_superadmin.sql
```

Default login: `superadmin` / `SuperAdmin123!` — change after first login.

## 5. Set secrets (production)

```bash
npx wrangler secret put NEXTAUTH_SECRET
# paste a long random string (openssl rand -base64 32)

npx wrangler secret put NEXTAUTH_URL
# paste your deployed URL, e.g. https://betting-data-pwa.your-subdomain.workers.dev
```

Optional seed overrides:

```bash
npx wrangler secret put SEED_SUPER_ADMIN_USERNAME
npx wrangler secret put SEED_SUPER_ADMIN_PASSWORD
```

## 6. Preview locally (Workers runtime)

```bash
cp .dev.vars.example .dev.vars
# edit .dev.vars — set NEXTAUTH_SECRET

npx wrangler d1 migrations apply betting-data-db --local
node scripts/generate-seed-sql.mjs
npx wrangler d1 execute betting-data-db --local --file=scripts/seed_superadmin.sql

npm run preview:cf
```

Open **http://localhost:8787** (Wrangler default).

## 7. Deploy

```bash
npm run deploy:cf
```

Wrangler prints your live URL (e.g. `https://betting-data-pwa.<account>.workers.dev`).

Update `NEXTAUTH_URL` secret to match that URL exactly (including `https`).

## 8. Git integration (optional)

1. Push code to GitHub/GitLab
2. Cloudflare Dashboard → **Workers & Pages** → **Create** → connect repo
3. Build command: `npm run deploy:cf` or use Workers Builds with:
   - Build: `npx opennextjs-cloudflare build`
   - Deploy via Wrangler in CI

Set the same secrets in the dashboard under **Settings → Variables**.

## Local dev vs Cloudflare

| Command | Use case |
|---------|----------|
| `npm run dev` | Fast Next.js dev (port 5000, local SQLite file) |
| `npm run preview:cf` | Test in real Workers runtime + local D1 |
| `npm run deploy:cf` | Production deploy |

## PWA on Cloudflare

The PWA (manifest + service worker) is included. Users can install from Chrome/Edge on your `https://` Workers URL.

## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Fix |
|-------|-----|
| `database_id` invalid | Run `wrangler d1 create` and update `wrangler.jsonc` |
| Auth redirect loop | `NEXTAUTH_URL` must match deployed URL exactly |
| 500 on API routes | Check D1 migrations applied (`--remote`) and seed ran |
| Build fails | Node 18+, delete `.open-next` and rebuild |

## Files added for Cloudflare

- `wrangler.jsonc` — Worker + D1 binding
- `open-next.config.ts` — OpenNext adapter config
- `migrations/` — D1 SQL migrations
- `src/lib/prisma.ts` — D1 adapter via `getPrisma()`

    "cf-typegen": "wrangler types --env-interface CloudflareEnv cloudflare-env.d.ts",
    "db:migrate:local": "wrangler d1 migrations apply betting-data-db --local",
    "db:migrate:remote": "wrangler d1 migrations apply betting-data-db --remote",
    "db:seed:sql": "node scripts/generate-seed-sql.mjs",
    "db:seed:remote": "node scripts/generate-seed-sql.mjs && wrangler d1 execute betting-data-db --remote --file=scripts/seed_superadmin.sql",
    "start": "next start -p 5000",

# serwist
public/sw.js
public/sw.js.map
public/swe-worker*

# cloudflare
.open-next
.wrangler
.dev.vars
scripts/seed_superadmin.sql

## Deploy to Cloudflare

See **[DEPLOY_CLOUDFLARE.md](./DEPLOY_CLOUDFLARE.md)** for full steps (D1 database, Wrangler, secrets, `npm run deploy:cf`).

Quick summary:

```bash
npx wrangler login
npx wrangler d1 create betting-data-db   # copy database_id into wrangler.jsonc
npm run db:migrate:remote
npm run db:seed:remote
npx wrangler secret put NEXTAUTH_SECRET
npx wrangler secret put NEXTAUTH_URL
npm run deploy:cf
```

## Using PostgreSQL / Supabase

content.text:
Cloudflare deployment is configured. Here’s what was added and how to deploy.

## What changed

| Piece | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| **OpenNext** (`@opennextjs/cloudflare`) | Runs Next.js 15 on Cloudflare Workers |
| **D1** (`wrangler.jsonc` + `migrations/`) | Edge database (replaces local SQLite in production) |
| **`getPrisma()`** | Uses D1 on Cloudflare, file SQLite locally |
| **`DEPLOY_CLOUDFLARE.md`** | Full deployment guide |

## Deploy steps

### 1. Install & log in

```powershell
cd C:\Users\Abex\.cursor\projects\empty-window
npm install
npx wrangler login
```

### 2. Create D1 database

```powershell
npx wrangler d1 create betting-data-db
```

Copy the **`database_id`** into `wrangler.jsonc` (replace `REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_D1_DATABASE_ID`).

### 3. Migrate & seed database

```powershell
npm run db:migrate:remote
npm run db:seed:remote
```

### 4. Set secrets

```powershell
npx wrangler secret put NEXTAUTH_SECRET
npx wrangler secret put NEXTAUTH_URL
```

For `NEXTAUTH_URL`, use your real Workers URL after the first deploy (e.g. `https://betting-data-pwa.<account>.workers.dev`).

### 5. Deploy

```powershell
npm run deploy:cf
```

### 6. Fix auth URL (if needed)

If login fails after deploy, set `NEXTAUTH_URL` again to the **exact** URL Wrangler prints.

---

## Commands reference

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm run dev` | Local dev (port 5000, SQLite file) |
| `npm run preview:cf` | Test on Workers locally (port 8787) |
| `npm run deploy:cf` | Deploy to Cloudflare |
| `npm run db:migrate:remote` | Apply schema to production D1 |
| `npm run db:seed:remote` | Create super admin on D1 |

Default login after seed: **`superadmin`** / **`SuperAdmin123!`**

Full details: **`DEPLOY_CLOUDFLARE.md`**.

**Note:** PWA install works on your `https://` Workers URL. Local `npm run dev` still uses port 5000 with a file database; only production uses D1.

[REDACTED]

Type error: 'body' is of type 'unknown'.

> 28 |   if (typeof body.canEditEntries === "boolean") {

Next.js build worker exited with code: 1 and signal: null

Adding a Zod schema for the PATCH user body to fix the TypeScript error.

[REDACTED]

Fixed. `req.json()` is typed as `unknown` in Next.js 15, so accessing `body.canEditEntries` directly failed the build.

**Change:** Added `patchUserSchema` in `validations.ts` and validated the PATCH body with `safeParse` before use (same pattern as your other API routes).

Redeploy:

```powershell
npm run deploy:cf
```

If the build reports another `unknown` body error, paste it here and we can fix those routes the same way.
